assertions.description: Output includes all six sections: Data Source, Fields to Collect (with example values), Output Schema, Scope and Frequency, Technical Considerations, Legal and Ethical Notes
Every field to be collected is named with a description and example value
Legal and ethical notes address robots.txt status and terms of service

assertion_results.note: All six sections present with court-specific detail
9 fields each with description and example (case number, judge, etc.)
robots.txt and ToS addressed; public interest basis noted
Legal notes address government data accessibility
All six sections with EU-specific legal considerations
10 fields including EU-specific identifiers with examples
GDPR and EU data access regulations addressed alongside robots.txt

methodology: Binary assertion testing: 3 inputs (standard, minimal, European context) x 3 assertions per skill. Each assertion graded pass/fail based on whether generated output satisfies the requirement.
